lesson.menu_desc: First conditional + urgency for critical escalations -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Escalations Under Pressure
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 9 -- Escalations Under Pressure

listenings.text: Hi Patrícia, this is David from the Atlas team. I am sorry to call you so late, but this is critical. Our main dashboard has been down for almost an hour, and the whole sales team cannot work. The impact is huge, and the downtime is costing us money every minute. I really need this escalated right now. If we do not have a fix tonight, we will miss our quarterly deadline tomorrow morning. Please tell me what the plan is. As soon as you have any news from your engineers, call me back. I will keep my phone on. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hi Patrícia, it is Sofia, the on-call engineering lead, with the escalation update you asked for. Good news: we have raised this to severity one and the whole team is on it now. Here is the plan. As soon as we confirm the root cause, we will push a fix to the main server. Unless something unexpected happens, the dashboard will be back within two hours, so we will beat David's deadline. The impact is serious, but it is under control. I have made a clear commitment to David's account: a full update every thirty minutes until the system is stable. If the fix does not work on the first try, we will roll back immediately and protect his data. Please reassure him that we are treating this as critical.
